title: hypericum humifusum var. liottardii
intro: Hypericum humifusum var. liottardii, also known as Creeping St. John's Wort, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its yellow flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin compounds. The health benefits of Creeping St. John's Wort include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for skin issues and infections. In herbal preparations, Creeping St. John's Wort is often used in tinctures, salves, and infusions, and can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally in moderation.
